evals.prompt: Tokyo 5-day itinerary from JSON → Notion payloads
evals.expected_output: Complete 5-day Tokyo Notion page with cover, database, daily sections, checklist
assertions: Page payload includes cover image URL and emoji icon
Page payload includes a Trip Overview callout block with destination, dates, travelers
POI names are bilingual (English + Japanese/Chinese) throughout the page blocks
An inline database is created with at least these columns: POI, Date, Category, Time Slot, Budget, Status, Location
Database has correct number of rows (11 POIs + 6 meals = 17 rows)
Daily sections use H2 headings with day number and theme
Packing checklist uses to_do block type with at least 8 items
Travel tips appear as callout blocks
Google Maps links are included as bookmark blocks for POIs
Blocks exceeding 100 are batched into separate API calls

evals.prompt: Seoul 2-day itinerary with null budget_jpy → Notion payloads
evals.expected_output: 2-day Seoul page handling null budgets gracefully, Korean bilingual names
assertions: Page payload includes cover image and emoji icon
Null budget_jpy values are not rendered as 0 — they are omitted or skipped entirely
Budget (TWD) values are correctly shown where available
POI names include Korean script (한글)
Database has 6 rows (4 POIs + 2 meals)
Packing checklist uses to_do blocks
Daily sections have H2 headings with bilingual themes

evals.prompt: Osaka 3-day (no JSON input) → generate itinerary + Notion payloads
evals.expected_output: Generated Osaka itinerary JSON + Notion payloads focused on food and Shinsaibashi
assertions: A canonical itinerary JSON file is generated following the skill schema format
Itinerary focuses on food/culinary POIs and Shinsaibashi/Dotonbori area
Generated JSON includes bilingual names (Traditional Chinese + Japanese)
Budget estimates in both JPY and TWD are included
Notion page payload is generated from the itinerary data
An inline database is created with POI rows
Packing checklist and travel tips are included
